questions.prompt: What is a branch in Git?
options: A backup copy stored on a remote server
A separate line of work that lets you make commits without touching main
A command that deletes uncommitted changes
Another name for a single commit
questions.explanation: A branch is a movable pointer to a line of commits. It lets you work in isolation while the main branch stays stable until you merge.

questions.prompt: Why do testers keep each new test or fix on its own feature branch?
options: Because Git refuses to commit directly to main
To isolate the work so main stays releasable until the change is ready and reviewed
Because branches make tests run faster
To avoid having to write commit messages
questions.explanation: A feature branch isolates one piece of work. Main stays stable and shippable, and the change only joins it once finished and merged.

questions.prompt: What does merging a feature branch into main do?
options: It deletes the feature branch and its history
It combines the feature branch's commits into main so both sets of changes are together
It copies main onto a remote server
It reverts main to an earlier commit
questions.explanation: Merging brings the commits from one branch into another. After merging your branch into main, main contains your changes plus everything else.

options: Two branches changed the same lines and Git cannot decide which to keep
You forgot to write a commit message
A test failed during the merge
questions.explanation: A conflict happens when both branches edited the same lines. Git marks the spot and asks you to choose the correct result, then add and commit the resolution.
